event_preview:
The Red Bull Ring is a 4.318 km (2.682 mi) permanent road course in Spielberg, Styria, set against the backdrop of the Styrian hills at an altitude of approximately 700 metres. The 10-corner layout splits into two distinct halves — a high-speed opening sector built around a long main straight and heavy braking at Turns 3 and 4, and a more flowing second half that requires a combination of commitment and precision. The circuit has a history of exposing who is ready and who isn't early in a season, and results here have repeatedly identified drivers to watch for the remainder of the year.

The Red Bull Ring hosts the opening round of the 2026 FIA Formula Regional European Championship — the first season under full FIA governance after the series previously operated as the Formula Regional European Championship by Alpine. The championship introduces the all-new Tatuus T-326, developed in partnership with the FIA and powered by Toyota engines on Pirelli tyres, replacing the Renault-engined T-318. Ten teams field three cars each for a 30-car grid across an eight-round calendar. The Red Bull Ring is one of four venues running the three-race format this year, with the second race of the weekend using a reversed grid for the top twelve from Qualifying 1. A new points system awards bonus points for pole position and the fastest lap recorded inside the top ten.

Williams-backed Prema Racing driver Kean Nakamura-Berta arrives as the pre-season favourite, having won the Italian F4 championship in 2025 and also finished second in the Middle East series this winter. Rashid Al Dhaheri of R-ace GP — Nakamura-Berta's main rival in the Middle East series — and Sebastian Wheldon of MP Motorsport are among those expected to challenge, while Jules Roussel of CL Motorsport brings the momentum of winning last November's FIA F4 World Cup in Macau.
